behaviorsAudited: preserves existing AgenC-side LSP open, change, save, and close notifications for inline fallback buffers
keeps LSP notification failures from breaking inline editing or changing visible buffer status
marks inline fallback as the only provider with AgenC-side LSP passthrough
marks embedded Neovim as not claiming AgenC-side LSP passthrough until a real Neovim LSP bridge exists

edgeCasesAudited: opening an inline fallback buffer notifies the LSP bridge with absolute path and content
editing inline fallback text notifies the LSP bridge with the updated document text
saving and closing inline fallback buffers notify the LSP bridge in order
a thrown LSP notification does not revert inline buffer edits or set an error state
embedded Neovim capabilities explicitly report LSP passthrough as unavailable

commandResults: PASS: 1 test file passed, 3 tests passed, duration 5.01s
sourceEvidence: TODO.md:20-23 identifies BufferStore as the current owner of file open/save, dirty-state checks, and LSP notifications.
TODO.md:57-64 requires provider capability reporting, including lspPassthrough.
TODO.md:120-132 requires the Neovim path to notify the existing LSP bridge only if that provider remains responsible for AgenC-side LSP state.
behaviorEvidence: Inline fallback delegates open/save/close/input editing to WorkbenchBufferStore in runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/providers/inline/InlineBufferProvider.ts:51-64 and :94-100; WorkbenchBufferStore imports the existing LSP bridge functions in runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/BufferStore.ts:61-68.

Open/change/save/close notifications are implemented in runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/BufferStore.ts:847, :769-772, :240, and :193, with previous-file close on file replacement in :836-838; the contract test asserts open/change/save/close calls in runtime/tests/tui/workbench/buffer-lsp-bridge.contract.test.ts:37-53.

LSP notification failures are isolated by safeNotifyBufferLsp in runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/BufferStore.ts:949-955, and the underlying bridge also catches async failures in runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/lsp.ts:18-45; the contract test throws from notifyBufferLspChanged and asserts retained text plus ready/null-error status in runtime/tests/tui/workbench/buffer-lsp-bridge.contract.test.ts:56-73.

Inline LSP passthrough is true in runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/providers/types.ts:102-110 and applied to InlineBufferProvider identity in runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/providers/inline/InlineBufferProvider.ts:28-35; the assertion is in runtime/tests/tui/workbench/buffer-lsp-bridge.contract.test.ts:76-78.

Embedded Neovim LSP passthrough is false in runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/providers/types.ts:112-120 and applied to NeovimBufferProvider identity in runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/providers/neovim/NeovimBufferProvider.ts:60-72; the assertion is in runtime/tests/tui/workbench/buffer-lsp-bridge.contract.test.ts:76-78.
